title: From Blocks to Real Code
modules: What Happens When Steps Are Wrong?
Fix the Bug, Save the Day
synopsis: You're a game designer whose studio only has one rule: everything you imagine, you have to build yourself — starting with colorful blocks and ending with your first real lines of code. Each level of the story breaks if you don't fix the logic, teaching you to think like a programmer by actually being one.
rationale: Children aged 8-10 have high creative motivation but lack a bridge between visual block-based tools and the text-based coding that powers AI and real software, stalling their progression.
learning_outcome: Students can build a working Scratch project and write and explain a simple Python function that takes an input and returns an output.
source_signals: Show HN: PickCode – An educational coding environment for students after Scratch
